dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used for the existence of inanimate things, plants, events, or abstract things.

example.kana: つくえのうえにほんがある。
example.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga aru.
example.english: There is a book on the desk.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used for the existence of people and animals.

example.english: There is a cat in the garden.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: The everyday word for a cat.

example.english: There is a cat in the garden.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A garden or yard around a house or building.

example.english: There is a cat in the garden.

example.japanese: 机の上に本があります。
example.kana: つくえのうえにほんがあります。
example.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
example.english: There is a book on the desk.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A position word meaning above or on top.

example.japanese: 机の上に本があります。
example.kana: つくえのうえにほんがあります。
example.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
example.english: There is a book on the desk.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Time as something you have, need, or spend.
A meeting where people discuss work, school, or plans.

example.kana: あしたかいぎがあります。

exercises.title: あります vs います — Core Forms
exercises.intro: Japanese uses two different existence verbs depending on what exists. Use あります for objects, plants, places, events, and abstract things; use います for people, animals, insects, and other beings that move on their own.
rows: there is not / do not have
there was not / did not have
there is not / do not have
exercises.takeaway: Choose あります for inanimate things and います for living beings that move on their own.

exercises.prompt: Which verb fits a cat in the garden?
Say 'There is a cat in the garden' using the animate existence verb:

slot.hint: The polite existence verb for people and animals.

exercises.translation: There is a cat in the garden.
exercises.speakText: にわに ねこが います。

exercises.title: Existence at a Location
exercises.intro: To introduce something new at a location, put the place first with に, then the thing or person with が. This pattern answers 'What is there?'
rows: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
There is a book on the desk.
この まちに びょういんが みっつ あります。
kono machi ni byouin ga mittsu arimasu.
There are three hospitals in this town.
となりの へやに ひとが います。
tonari no heya ni hito ga imasu.
There is a person in the next room.
exercises.takeaway: Use PLACE に + THING/PERSON が + あります/います to introduce what exists there.

exercises.prompt: Which pattern introduces a new thing at a place?
Say 'There is a book on the desk' by filling the object:
exercises.translation: There is a book on the desk.
exercises.speakText: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。

exercises.prompt: Say 'The toilet is on the second floor' using the known-topic order:

slot.hint: The floor above the first floor.

exercises.translation: The toilet is on the second floor.
exercises.speakText: トイレは にかいに あります。

exercises.title: Possession, Events, and Abstract Things
exercises.intro: あります can also mean 'have' when the thing you have is inanimate or abstract. Events and plans also use あります because they are treated as occurrences, not living beings.
rows: きょうだいが います。
じかんが ありません。
あした かいぎが あります。
パーティーが あります。
よていが あります。
exercises.takeaway: Use います for living people in your life, but use あります for objects, money, time, plans, and events.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase means 'I don't have time'?
options: じかんが ありません。
じかんが いません。
きょうだいが あります。

exercises.prompt: Say 'There is a meeting tomorrow' by filling the event word:

slot.hint: A work or school event where people discuss something.

exercises.speakText: あした かいぎが あります。

exercises.prompt: Say 'I have siblings' using the animate possession verb:

slot.hint: The polite existence verb for living people.

exercises.speakText: きょうだいが います。

exercises.title: Location Words with の
exercises.intro: Japanese position words behave like nouns, so they need の after the thing they relate to. Say NOUN の POSITION に before the existence verb.
exercises.takeaway: Link a noun to a position word with の: つくえの うえに, not つくえ うえに.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase correctly means 'under the chair'?
Say 'There is a cat under the chair' by filling the position word:
patternJp: に ねこが います。

slot.hint: The position word meaning below or under.

exercises.translation: There is a cat under the chair.
exercises.speakText: いすの したに ねこが います。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'There is a cat in the garden'?
exercises.title: あります vs います — the core rule

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: にわに ねこが います。
variants.en: There is a cat in the garden.
variants.note: Cats move on their own, so the animate existence verb is correct.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.jp: にわに ねこが あります。
variants.en: (intended: There is a cat in the garden.)
variants.note: あります is for inanimate things, not living creatures.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: にわが ねこに います。
variants.note: The location takes に, and the existing being takes が.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: にわに ほんが あります。
variants.en: There is a book in the garden.
variants.note: This sentence is grammatical, but it talks about a book, not a cat.

exercises.takeaway: Use います for people and animals; use あります for inanimate things.
exercises.speakText: にわに ねこが います。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence introduces a new book on the desk?
exercises.title: Two sentence orders — same meaning, different focus

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
variants.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
variants.en: There is a book on the desk.
variants.note: PLACE に THING が introduces the thing as new information.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: ほんは つくえの うえに あります。
variants.romaji: hon wa tsukue no ue ni arimasu.
variants.en: The book is on the desk.
variants.note: This answers where a known book is, rather than introducing a new book.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: つくえは ほんに あります。
variants.note: This reverses the location and the thing that exists.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.jp: つくえの うえに ほんは あります。
variants.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon wa arimasu.
variants.note: Use が when introducing the book as new information.

exercises.takeaway: PLACE に THING が introduces what exists; THING は PLACE に tells where a known thing is.
exercises.speakText: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses あります for an event?
exercises.title: あります for events and abstract possession

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: パーティーが あります。
variants.note: Events use あります because they are inanimate occurrences.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.jp: パーティーが います。
variants.note: います is for people and animals, not events.

variants.label: NATURAL
variants.jp: くるまが あります。
variants.note: This is natural possession with an inanimate object, but it is not the event sentence asked for.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: かいぎに あります。
variants.note: The event should be marked with が: かいぎが あります.

exercises.takeaway: Events, schedules, money, time, and plans use あります.
exercises.speakText: パーティーが あります。

exercises.prompt: Which phrase correctly says 'It is on top of the desk'?
exercises.title: Location words (NOUN の + position word)

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: つくえの うえに あります。
variants.en: It is on top of the desk.
variants.note: Use の to link the noun to the position word.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.jp: つくえ うえに あります。
variants.note: Position words need の after the noun: つくえの うえ.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: つくえの したに あります。
variants.note: This is grammatical, but した means under, not on top.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: つくえの うえが あります。
variants.note: The location phrase needs に when saying where something exists.

exercises.takeaway: For position words, use NOUN の POSITION に before あります or います.
exercises.speakText: つくえの うえに あります。

exercises.title: Sentences in Context
exercises.intro: These are the main existence sentences from the lesson. Notice how が introduces what exists, は marks a known topic, and に marks the location.
rows: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
There is a book on the desk.
トイレは にかいに あります。
The toilet is on the second floor.
この まちに びょういんが みっつ あります。
kono machi ni byouin ga mittsu arimasu.
There are three hospitals in this town.
にわに ねこが います。
There is a cat in the garden.
きょうだいが います。
じかんが ありません。
あした かいぎが あります。
exercises.takeaway: Existence grammar also covers possession, events, and everyday location sentences.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
options: There is a book on the desk.
There is a desk in the book.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: にわに ねこが います。
options: There is a cat in the garden.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: じかんが ありません。
options: I have a lot of time.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: あした かいぎが あります。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: となりの へやに ひとが います。
options: Nobody is in the next room.
There is a person in the next room.
I went to the next room.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: おかねが ない！

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: きょうだいが います。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: この まちに びょういんが みっつ あります。
options: There is one hospital in this town.
There are three hospitals in this town.
The hospital is far from here.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its meaning:

cards.jp: つくえの うえに ほんが あります。
cards.romaji: tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
cards.en: There is a book on the desk.

cards.jp: にわに ねこが います。
cards.en: There is a cat in the garden.

cards.jp: トイレは にかいに あります。
cards.en: The toilet is on the second floor.

cards.jp: きょうだいが います。
じかんが ありません。
あした かいぎが あります。
いすの したに ねこが います。
cards.romaji: isu no shita ni neko ga imasu.
cards.en: There is a cat under the chair.

cards.jp: この まちに びょういんが みっつ あります。
cards.romaji: kono machi ni byouin ga mittsu arimasu.
cards.en: There are three hospitals in this town.

cards.jp: となりの へやに ひとが います。
cards.romaji: tonari no heya ni hito ga imasu.
cards.en: There is a person in the next room.
